patches.summary: Register the hand-built transcendence layer (advise, compare, advise-replay, cost-trace, budget) in the generated entrypoints root.go and which.go.
patches.reason: The advisor package (internal/advisor/*) and its five novel commands are hand-authored beyond the generator's endpoint mirror (tracked in .printing-press.json novel_features). The only edits to generator-owned files are the AddCommand registrations in root.go and the curated whichIndex entries in which.go that wire those novel commands into the CLI.
patches.validated_outcome: go build ./... ; ollama-cloud-pp-cli advise/compare/advise-replay/budget/cost-trace --help all resolve; cli-printing-press verify-skill --dir . passes

patches.summary: Recommendation.Fallback picks the first live candidate that differs from the recommendation.
patches.reason: PR #805 review (advisor logic): fallback was hardcoded to live[1], which equals the recommendation once the tiebreaker promotes live[1] to winner (the len>=3 guard does not fire with exactly two live models), leaving a routing retry with no escape path.

patches.summary: divergence_pct is tallied over all rows in the window, independent of --diverge-only / --limit.
patches.reason: PR #805 review: with --diverge-only the displayed set contained only diverged rows, so the rate was trivially 100%. The denominator is now the total rows in the window.

patches.summary: compare drops the never-populated est_cost_usd field and the 'cost' claim from every surface, emits prompt_hash (matching the advisor log), surfaces --prompt-file read errors, and honors --timeout via a per-request context.
patches.reason: PR #805 review: compare advertised side-by-side cost it never produced, documented a prompt_hash absent from output, swallowed prompt-read errors behind a generic message, and ignored --timeout.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... ; grep confirms no 'cost' claim remains on any compare surface

patches.summary: Prior generated-file patches from the initial 4.2.0 print are no longer needed — the 4.19.0 machine fixes them natively.
patches.reason: PR #805 also flagged a non-deterministic cache key, a rate-limiter Wait() TOCTOU, discarded per-request context deadlines, and a silent token-refresh no-op — all in generator-owned files. The 4.19.0 reprint regenerates those files with the fixes built in (cacheKey is fully reworked, the client API is context-first, AdaptiveLimiter.Wait reserves its slot under the lock, refresh paths error explicitly), so no hand-edit to generated code remains for them.
patches.validated_outcome: Verified by inspection of regenerated internal/client/client.go and internal/cliutil/ratelimit.go
